prompt: Wide architectural three-quarter exterior view of a dusty white pickup truck parked nose-in at the driveway of a 1980s New England colonial home, vinyl siding, brick chimney, autumn-yellowing maple trees overhead. The truck has a magnetic side decal panel that is clearly branded but completely out of focus and unreadable, ladders strapped to a black headache rack, a few pieces of trim lumber sticking out the bed. The driver-side door slightly open, a leather work boot stepping down. Shot with a 35mm prime lens at low three-quarter angle, very early morning soft golden side-light raking across the brick and vinyl, mist still on the lawn — 7am arrival mood, dependable, on-time. Slightly desaturated palette, a red toolbox in the truck bed is the only fully saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no readable logos.
